questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Young's double-slit experiment demonstrates the wave nature of light.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the interference pattern formed on the screen shows bright and dark fringes.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: In Young's double-slit experiment, the interference pattern formed on the screen is a result of the superposition of light waves passing through the two slits, demonstrating the wave nature of light.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The phenomenon of total internal reflection occurs when light passes from a denser to a rarer medium.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This happens when the angle of incidence is greater than the critical angle.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Total internal reflection occurs when light passes from a denser to a rarer medium at a large enough angle of incidence, and the critical angle is the angle beyond which total internal reflection occurs.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The diffraction pattern observed in a single-slit experiment is similar to the pattern observed in a double-slit experiment.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is due to the fact that both experiments involve the diffraction of light around obstacles.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: While both experiments do involve diffraction, the diffraction pattern observed in a single-slit experiment is not similar to the pattern observed in a double-slit experiment, as the latter also involves interference between the light waves passing through the two slits.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Polarization of light can be achieved by passing it through a polarizing filter.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The polarizing filter only allows light waves with a specific orientation to pass through.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Passing light through a polarizing filter allows only light waves with a specific orientation to pass through, effectively polarizing the light.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Brewster's angle is the angle of incidence at which the reflected light is completely polarized.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This angle is given by the equation tan θ_B = n2 / n1.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Brewster's angle is indeed the angle of incidence at which the reflected light is completely polarized, and it can be calculated using the equation tan θ_B = n2 / n1, where n1 and n2 are the refractive indices of the two media.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The phenomenon of diffraction is observed only when the obstacle size is comparable to the wavelength of light.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because diffraction is the bending of light around obstacles, which occurs when the obstacle is of the same order as the wavelength of light.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of diffraction is indeed observed when the obstacle size is comparable to the wavelength of light, as this allows for the bending of light around the obstacle.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In a double-slit experiment, the intensity of the central maximum is maximum when the slits are very far apart.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the distance between the slits has no effect on the intensity of the central maximum.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The intensity of the central maximum in a double-slit experiment is actually maximum when the slits are very close together, not when they are far apart.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The intensity of the diffracted light is maximum at the central maximum in a single-slit experiment.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because the diffracted light waves are in phase at the central maximum.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The intensity of the diffracted light is indeed maximum at the central maximum in a single-slit experiment, as the diffracted light waves are in phase at this point.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The resolving power of an optical instrument is its ability to distinguish between two closely spaced objects.
questions.reason: Reason (R): This is because a higher resolving power allows for the observation of smaller details.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A

(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The resolving power of an optical instrument is indeed its ability to distinguish between two closely spaced objects, as a higher resolving power allows for the observation of smaller details.
